questions.question: At what age was Akbar proclaimed emperor?
questions.explanation: Akbar was only 13 years old when he was proclaimed emperor of the Mughal Empire.

questions.question: For how many years did Akbar rule the Mughal Empire?
questions.explanation: Akbar ruled for 50 years, from 1556 to 1605 CE.

questions.question: Who served as Akbar's regent during his early years?
questions.explanation: Bairam Khan served as Akbar's regent and guided the administration until Akbar dismissed him at the age of 18.

questions.question: At what age did Akbar dismiss Bairam Khan?
questions.explanation: Akbar dismissed his regent Bairam Khan when he turned 18 and began ruling independently.

questions.question: In the Second Battle of Panipat (1556), Akbar defeated which rival?
questions.explanation: The Second Battle of Panipat in 1556 was fought between Akbar's forces and Hemu, who was defeated.

questions.question: Which province was annexed by Akbar in 1561?
questions.explanation: Akbar annexed Malwa in 1561 as part of his military conquests to expand the empire.

questions.question: When was Gujarat annexed by Akbar?
questions.explanation: Akbar annexed Gujarat in 1572 during his campaign to consolidate and expand the Mughal Empire.

questions.question: Akbar married the daughter of the Raja of which kingdom?
questions.explanation: Akbar married the daughter of the Raja of Jaipur (Amber) as part of his policy of building alliances with Rajput rulers.

questions.question: Who was the Rajput commander at the siege of Chittor?
questions.explanation: Jai Mal was the Rajput commander who defended Chittor when Akbar besieged and captured it.

questions.question: What did the Rajput women perform during the fall of Chittor?
questions.explanation: When Chittor fell to Akbar, the Rajput women performed jauhar (self-immolation) to preserve their honour.

questions.question: The Battle of Haldighati (1576) was fought between Rana Pratap and which Mughal general?
questions.explanation: The Battle of Haldighati in 1576 was fought between Rana Pratap of Mewar and the Mughal army led by Raja Man Singh.

questions.question: Akbar's empire was divided into how many subas (provinces)?
questions.explanation: Akbar divided his empire into 15 subas (provinces) for efficient administration.

questions.question: What was the correct order of administrative subdivisions under Akbar, from largest to smallest?
questions.explanation: The empire was divided into subas (provinces), which were further subdivided into sarkars, and then into parganas.

questions.question: In the Mansabdari system, what was the highest rank (in terms of horsemen)?
questions.explanation: In Akbar's Mansabdari system, the ranks ranged from 10 to 7000 horsemen.

questions.question: Which feature was characteristic of the Mansabdari system?
options: C. Only Muslims could hold ranks
D. Ranks were based on birth
questions.explanation: A key feature of the Mansabdari system was that the ranks were not hereditary and were assigned based on merit.

questions.question: Who was the wazir/diwan in Akbar's council, responsible for revenue?
questions.explanation: The wazir or diwan was the minister in charge of revenue in Akbar's council of ministers.

questions.question: Which minister in Akbar's council was responsible for military affairs?
questions.explanation: The Mir Bakshi was responsible for military administration in Akbar's council.

questions.question: Todar Mal's land revenue system used a measuring instrument called the bamboo jarib. What was special about it?
options: A. It was made of gold
B. It had iron rings to prevent shrinkage
C. It was exactly one mile long
D. It was imported from Persia
questions.correct_answer: B. It had iron rings to prevent shrinkage
questions.explanation: Todar Mal introduced the bamboo jarib with iron rings for land measurement, which prevented the bamboo from shrinking or expanding.

questions.question: Under Todar Mal's Bandobast, land was classified into how many categories?
questions.explanation: Todar Mal classified land into 4 categories as part of his systematic land revenue reforms.

questions.question: What fraction of the average produce was fixed as land revenue under Todar Mal's system?
questions.explanation: Under Todar Mal's Bandobast, the land revenue was fixed at one-third of the average produce.

questions.question: In which year was the Ibadat Khana built at Fatehpur Sikri?
questions.explanation: The Ibadat Khana (House of Worship) was built by Akbar in 1575 at Fatehpur Sikri for religious discussions.

questions.question: What was the Mahzar of 1579?
options: A. A new religion founded by Akbar
B. A declaration making Akbar the supreme religious authority
questions.correct_answer: B. A declaration making Akbar the supreme religious authority
questions.explanation: The Mahzar of 1579 was a document that gave Akbar the authority to be the supreme arbiter in religious matters.

questions.question: Who was the only Hindu to accept Akbar's Din-i-Ilahi?
questions.explanation: Birbal was the only Hindu who accepted Akbar's new faith, Din-i-Ilahi (Divine Faith), founded in 1582.

questions.question: What was the gold coin during Akbar's reign called?
questions.explanation: The gold coin during Akbar's reign was called a mohur.

questions.question: Who wrote the Akbarnama?
questions.explanation: Abul Fazl was the court historian who wrote the Akbarnama, a detailed account of Akbar's reign.
